title: The One Where The Monkey Gets Away
weakest_section: 21:00-22:00 range with romantic setup having fewer laughs

jokes.text: You're not the guy that asked for tea, are you?
jokes.setup: Long detailed list of tea varieties creates expectation of helpful service
jokes.punchline: Reveals they've been helping the wrong person entirely
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with good economy - builds expectation then subverts it efficiently

jokes.text: Oh, good. Because where else would we get any?
jokes.setup: Character excited about free coffee sample
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response pointing out they work at/frequent a coffee place
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcasm about obvious irony - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, right.
jokes.setup: Sarcastic comment about coffee abundance
jokes.punchline: Delayed realization creates awkward beat
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing character obliviousness

jokes.text: Barry, who you almost... And Mindy, your maid of... Let me see. That's Mindy? She is pretty! Lucky... ...to have had a friend like you.
jokes.setup: Monica struggles to be diplomatic about Rachel's ex and former maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Awkward recovery from almost calling Barry lucky to verbal gymnastics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's caring awkwardness in delicate situation

jokes.text: He's mastered the difference between 'bring me the' and 'pee in the.'
jokes.setup: Ross training Marcel to bring rice
jokes.punchline: Reveals the monkey's previous bathroom mistakes with wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good structure - the similarity of commands makes the mistake believable and funny

jokes.text: 'Bring me the' and... Rach?
jokes.setup: Ross explaining monkey training
jokes.punchline: Rachel's silence/distraction creates awkward beat
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction showing character distraction

jokes.text: Whoa, what... What happened to 'Forget relationships! I'm done with men!' The whole penis embargo?
jokes.setup: Rachel changing her stance on relationships
jokes.punchline: 'Penis embargo' - unexpected formal term for swearing off men
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining political/economic terminology with sexual content - unexpectedly formal language for casual topic

jokes.text: In fact, it's funny. Often, someone you wouldn't think could... ...curl your toes... ...might just be the one who... ...gets interrupted.
jokes.setup: Monica building to revelation about Ross
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off just as she's about to reveal feelings
jokes.explanation: Good structural timing - builds expectation then subverts with interruption at crucial moment

jokes.text: I'm sorry it wasn't one of those movies with guns and bombs... ...and buses going really fast!
jokes.setup: Guys complaining about chick flick
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top description of stereotypical action movie elements
jokes.explanation: Standard genre satire - competent but predictable

jokes.text: I don't need violence in a movie... ...as long as there's a little nudity. -There was nudity. -I meant female nudity. I don't need to see Lou Grant frolicking.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining his movie preferences
jokes.punchline: 'Lou Grant frolicking' - absurd mental image of serious TV character
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor with unexpected word choice - 'frolicking' creates absurd visual

jokes.text: Hugh! Hugh Grant!
jokes.setup: Chandler says 'Lou Grant'
jokes.punchline: Monica corrects to Hugh Grant, revealing the mistake
jokes.explanation: Simple mistaken identity correction - the previous joke was funnier with wrong name

jokes.text: I can't believe we're discussing this. -I agree. I'm, like, in disbelief.
jokes.setup: Joey expressing disbelief
jokes.punchline: Chandler mockingly repeats the same sentiment with Joey's vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's tendency to mock Joey's speech patterns

jokes.text: She said she wants a relationship with someone exactly like me. She said that? I added the 'exactly like me' part.
jokes.setup: Ross claims Rachel wants someone exactly like him
jokes.punchline: Admits he fabricated the key part of the statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Ross's hopeful delusion and honesty about his wishful thinking

jokes.text: I can't remember the last time a girl took care of my monkey.
jokes.setup: Ross talking about Rachel babysitting Marcel
jokes.punchline: Double entendre with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual double entendre - functional but relies on obvious innuendo

jokes.text: Know what you should do? Take her back to the 1890s when that phrase was last used.
jokes.setup: Ross says he wants to 'woo' Rachel
jokes.punchline: Mocking the outdated romantic terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler snark with specific time period reference making Ross seem antiquated

jokes.text: Now, she used to be a man. Look, there's Raven. We hate her. We're glad she's dying.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining soap opera characters
jokes.punchline: Casual, matter-of-fact delivery of dark sentiment about character death
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's investment in soap operas with darkly casual tone

jokes.text: Marcel, did you poo in the shoe?
jokes.setup: Marcel playing with Monica's shoes
jokes.punchline: Discovery of the inevitable monkey bathroom accident
jokes.explanation: Predictable physical comedy callback but serves story function

jokes.text: Sorry, Barry. A little engagement gift. I'm sure you didn't register for that.
jokes.setup: Rachel dealing with monkey poop in shoe
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically addressing her ex-fiancé about the mess as wedding gift
jokes.explanation: Creative way to connect the mess to her earlier emotional state about Barry's engagement

jokes.text: Wait. He pooped in my shoe? Which one? I don't know. The left one! Which ones? Those little clunky Amish things you think go with everything.
jokes.setup: Monica learning about shoe incident
jokes.punchline: Rachel's brutal description of Monica's fashion sense
jokes.explanation: 'Clunky Amish things you think go with everything' is a perfectly specific and devastating fashion insult

jokes.text: Why is the air in here so negative?
jokes.setup: Everyone panicking about lost monkey
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's mystical interpretation of obvious tension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe line - new age interpretation of obvious situation

jokes.text: Rachel lost Marcel. Oh, no! How? -He pooped in my shoe. -Which one? The cute black ones I always wear. No, which one? The right or left? Because the left one is lucky.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking 'which one' about shoes
jokes.punchline: Reveals she meant which foot because left is lucky
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Phoebe's superstitious nature while others focus on material concerns

jokes.text: All right. You're a monkey. You're loose in the city. Where do you go? It's his first time out, so he'll do touristy things. I'll go to Cats. You go to the Russian Tea Room.
jokes.setup: Trying to think like a lost monkey
jokes.punchline: Assuming monkey will act like tourist and see Broadway shows
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating monkey like out-of-town visitor is perfectly ridiculous yet delivered seriously

jokes.text: You stay here and wait by the phone. Spray Lysol in my shoe and wait for Ross to kill you. Does anybody want to trade?
jokes.setup: Dividing up search tasks
jokes.punchline: Rachel's tasks are cleaning poop and facing Ross's wrath
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel got the worst job assignment

jokes.text: I left a Belgian waffle out here. Did you take it? -Why'd you leave it in the hall? -I wasn't ready for it.
jokes.setup: Mr. Heckles accusing them of taking his waffle
jokes.punchline: 'I wasn't ready for it' - treating waffle like emotional commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Heckles absurdism - treating breakfast food like it requires emotional preparation is brilliantly weird

jokes.text: Have you seen a monkey? I saw Regis Philbin once.
jokes.setup: Direct question about missing monkey
jokes.punchline: Non sequitur about celebrity sighting
jokes.explanation: Classic Heckles non sequitur - specific celebrity choice makes it funnier

jokes.text: He's a black capuchin monkey with a white face... ...with Russian dressing and pickles on the side.
jokes.setup: Describing Marcel to deli worker
jokes.punchline: Accidentally orders sandwich while describing monkey
jokes.explanation: Clever structural joke where panic causes misdirected speech patterns

jokes.text: Okay, but before we head off to the murder capital of the Northeast...
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests going to Newark
jokes.punchline: Ross's dramatic description of Newark's reputation
jokes.explanation: Standard geographic humor about Newark's crime reputation

jokes.text: I know. I'm sorry. It's partially my fault. I shouldn't have had you start with a monkey. You should have started with a pencil.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to comfort Rachel about losing Marcel
jokes.punchline: Suggests she's so incompetent she should start with inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Ross's attempt at comfort backfiring into insult about basic competency

jokes.text: You know, that was a complete misunderstanding. We thought we had a monkey, but we didn't. -Turned out it was a hat. -Cat!
jokes.setup: Trying to lie to Animal Control
jokes.punchline: Bad lying gets worse with hat/cat confusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of bad lying under pressure

jokes.text: Oh, my God! You'd put that poor little creature in jail?
jokes.setup: Learning about prison sentence for illegal animals
jokes.punchline: Phoebe thinks the monkey would go to jail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe misunderstanding - thinks punishment applies to animal not owner

jokes.text: Remember how we talked about saying things quietly to yourself first? Yes, but there isn't always time.
jokes.setup: Monica referencing previous advice to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical excuse for blurting things out
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Phoebe's impulsive nature despite good intentions

jokes.text: Would it have been hard to say, 'Morning, Luisa'... ...or 'Nice overalls'?
jokes.setup: Luisa confronting Monica about high school
jokes.punchline: Specific example of compliment she wanted about unfashionable clothing
jokes.explanation: 'Nice overalls' as desired compliment reveals her fashion struggles and social desperation

jokes.text: Oh, I'm so sorry! It's not so much you. You were fat. You had your own problems. But you. What a bitch!
jokes.setup: Luisa explaining why she's upset with them
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about Monica's weight vs. Rachel's personality
jokes.explanation: Shockingly direct insult with good structure - dismisses Monica's issue as circumstantial but attacks Rachel's character

jokes.text: Go, Bobcats! I could. But I won't. If I find that monkey, he's mine.
jokes.setup: Monica appealing to school spirit
jokes.punchline: Luisa uses school cheer then immediately betrays them
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of school loyalty appeal with immediate betrayal

jokes.text: -A monkey. -Yes, have you seen any? No, I haven't seen a monkey. Can you fix radiators?
jokes.setup: Looking for monkey at apartment building
jokes.punchline: Woman immediately changes subject to practical household problem
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection showing priorities clash

jokes.text: Sure! Did you try turning the knob back the other way? -Of course! -Oh. Then, no.
jokes.setup: Chandler confidently offering to help with radiator
jokes.punchline: Admits he has no other ideas after basic suggestion fails
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of male confidence about home repair skills

jokes.text: We may not know about radiators... ...but we do have expertise in the heating and cooling milieu.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to impress women after radiator failure
jokes.punchline: Uses unnecessarily fancy word 'milieu' incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment trying to sound smart with vocabulary above his level

jokes.text: Aren't we in the middle of something? But these women are very hot, and they need our help. And they're very hot!
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to stay focused on finding Marcel
jokes.punchline: Joey repeats 'very hot' immediately for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Basic repetition showing Joey's single-minded focus on attractive women

jokes.text: He's this high and answers to the name Marcel. So if we could get pictures of you, it'd help us out.
jokes.setup: Joey describing lost monkey to women
jokes.punchline: Somehow connects monkey search to getting photos of attractive women
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey logic failure trying to hit on women during emergency

jokes.text: From now on, you don't get to talk to other people.
jokes.setup: Joey's completely inappropriate behavior with women
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating Joey like misbehaving child
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response treating adult like child who lost privileges

jokes.text: Oh, it's okay. It was just my left leg.
jokes.setup: Phoebe feels something brush against her leg
jokes.punchline: Realizes it was her own other leg
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy showing Phoebe's spatial confusion

jokes.text: Oh, gosh!
jokes.setup: Phoebe gets hit with tranquilizer dart
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to being shot
jokes.explanation: Understated reaction to dramatic event is perfectly Phoebe

jokes.text: It's cold, it's dark. He doesn't know the Village! And now I have a broken foot. I have no monkey and a broken foot!
jokes.setup: Ross panicking about Marcel being lost
jokes.punchline: Adds his own injury to list of disasters
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of complaints showing Ross's self-pity

jokes.text: You want me to break my foot too? I'll break it right now. Oh, my God! There, are you happy now? Yeah, now that you kicked the sign... ...I don't miss Marcel anymore!
jokes.setup: Rachel offering to break her foot too out of guilt
jokes.punchline: Accidentally does it, Ross sarcastically says it helped
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with good timing - intention becomes reality with sarcastic response

jokes.text: It's not like I did this on purpose. No, this is just vintage Rachel. Things just sort of happen around you! You're off in 'Rachel Land,' doing your 'Rachel Thing'... ...oblivious to people's monkeys or to people's feelings and...
jokes.setup: Rachel defending herself
jokes.punchline: Ross's specific description of Rachel's obliviousness including 'people's monkeys'
jokes.explanation: 'Rachel Land' and 'Rachel Thing' are specific character attacks, 'people's monkeys' is absurdly specific complaint

jokes.text: Hey, banana man!
jokes.setup: Seeing man with bananas who might have Marcel
jokes.punchline: Calling him 'banana man' based on obvious visual
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor based on obvious visual cue

jokes.text: This is so intense! One side of my butt is totally asleep and the other side has no idea.
jokes.setup: Phoebe affected by tranquilizer dart
jokes.punchline: Specific description of partial numbness with personification
jokes.explanation: Great specific physical description with personification - each butt cheek having separate awareness is absurd

jokes.text: -Then what's with the bananas? Potassium.
jokes.setup: Suspicious about bananas if he doesn't have monkey
jokes.punchline: Completely practical health-conscious answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response deflating conspiracy theory with health facts

jokes.text: Are you insane? Come here, Marcel. Come here, Patty. Come here, Marcel. Come here, Patty. Here, monkey. Here, monkey!
jokes.setup: Dispute over monkey ownership
jokes.punchline: Both calling monkey by different names simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation showing competing claims through parallel calling

jokes.text: That's not my monkey. The dress is mine. Send it back whenever.
jokes.setup: Losing custody dispute over monkey
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's more concerned about getting his dress back
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - priorities shift from monkey to cross-dressing costume

jokes.text: All right, I want my monkey! -Oh, come on, Luisa. -Sorry, prom queen. You had to be a bitch in school, instead of fat.
jokes.setup: Ross demanding Marcel back from Luisa
jokes.punchline: Luisa's brutal analysis of high school social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback with brutal honesty about high school social choices

jokes.text: In high school, I was prom queen... ...and the homecoming queen and you... ...were also there.
jokes.setup: Rachel listing her high school achievements
jokes.punchline: Dismissive acknowledgment that Luisa existed but was irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Perfect mean girl dismissal - acknowledging existence while emphasizing irrelevance

jokes.text: Heckles! Can I have this? For the most part.
jokes.setup: Rachel calling Monica childish
jokes.punchline: Monica qualifying that they're adults 'for the most part'
jokes.explanation: Good qualifier acknowledging their behavior isn't consistently mature

jokes.text: All right. Then I'll call your supervisor and tell her... ...you shot my friend with a dart!
jokes.setup: Luisa refusing to help with monkey
jokes.punchline: Rachel's threat sounds ridiculous out of context
jokes.explanation: Absurd but technically accurate complaint that would sound insane to supervisor

jokes.text: It'll be nice to get this off, won't it? Or we can leave it on. That's fine. With the right pumps, that would be a great little outfit.
jokes.setup: Ross removing Marcel's dress
jokes.punchline: Suggests it could work as fashion choice with accessories
jokes.explanation: Mild fashion humor about monkey in drag being acceptable with right accessories

jokes.text: Are you in the mood for something grape?
jokes.setup: Ross offering wine to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Using 'grape' as adjective instead of wine/romantic
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay attempting to be suave but coming off awkward

jokes.text: The neighbors must be vacuuming.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment with loud sounds from upstairs
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation for obvious sexual sounds
jokes.explanation: Standard innocent misinterpretation of sexual sounds

jokes.text: Barry? I can't do it. I can't marry Mindy. I think I'm still in love with you.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel's romantic moment building
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by Rachel's ex declaring his feelings
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom timing - romantic moment interrupted by complication

jokes.text: We have got to start locking that door!
jokes.setup: Another unwanted interruption
jokes.punchline: Ross's practical solution to recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Practical response to sitcom convention of unlocked apartment doors

jokes.text: This is me in The Sound of Music. See the von Trapp kids? That's because I'm in front of them. Big nun! I thought that was an alp.
jokes.setup: Monica showing high school theater photo
jokes.punchline: Chandler's brutal observation about her size in costume
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual joke - mistaking person for geographic feature is perfectly cruel and specific

jokes.text: Well, I went to boarding school with 400 boys. Any sex I had would've involved a major lifestyle choice.
jokes.setup: Talking about high school sex experiences
jokes.punchline: Ross's euphemistic way of discussing homosexual implications
jokes.explanation: Good euphemistic humor about same-sex boarding school environment

jokes.text: My butt cheek is waking up!
jokes.setup: Earlier tranquilizer dart effects
jokes.punchline: Update on her ongoing numbness situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier physical comedy with personification of body parts
